name: Grade 6 Fishing & Water Skills — Semester 1
description: An 18-week Grade 6 pathway for analyzing systems, using disciplinary tools, and supporting claims through Fishing & Water Skills. Learning advances through inquiry, practice, authentic application, revision, and communication.
learningGoals: Develop grade 6 knowledge and transferable skills in analyzing systems, using disciplinary tools, and supporting claims through Fishing & Water Skills.
Use appropriate evidence, vocabulary, tools, and methods to explain reasoning.
Plan, revise, collaborate responsibly, and communicate a well-supported conclusion.

lessons.title: Launch inquiry: analyzing systems, using disciplinary tools, and supporting claims through Fishing & Water Skills
lessons.objective: I can develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about analyzing systems, using disciplinary tools, and supporting claims through Fishing & Water Skills and explain my reasoning with appropriate evidence.

activities.title: Question and evidence
activities.learnerAction: The learner develop a focused question and gather relevant evidence about analyzing systems, using disciplinary tools, and supporting claims through Fishing & Water Skills, documents evidence in an age-appropriate format, and follows applicable safety, ethics, and collaboration expectations.
